description: Baseline/Zero Point - Clean air reference or complete methane absence
Ultra-low methane presence - Natural background levels
Low-level methane detection - Easily detectable, no safety concerns
Low-moderate methane levels - Typical of minor natural gas presence
Moderate methane levels - Continue normal monitoring
Elevated background - Monitor for potential minor leaks
Notable presence - Verify sensor array consistency
Moderate concentration - Industrial process emissions level
Notable methane presence - Verify sensor readings
Elevated levels requiring attention - Monitor trends
High-normal range - Track rate of change
Warning threshold approach - Enhanced monitoring required
Upper caution range - Prepare for potential action
High warning level - Implement enhanced protocols
Elevated risk level - Safety procedures activation
Very high risk - Critical safety measures
Extreme caution level - Full safety protocol
Critical emergency level - Immediate evacuation consideration
Extreme lethal risk - Maximum emergency protocols
Ultimate danger level - Critical system failure
Extreme emergency level - Site isolation required
Critical failure state - Emergency services deployment
Peak danger concentration - Full emergency deployment
Maximum recorded level - Complete system shutdown
